claim: Kyutai STT and TTS are open-weight models supporting low-latency, local speech processing for English and French; both available on Hugging Face.
actionable: For speech applications requiring local inference, use Kyutai STT/TTS models from Hugging Face; avoids cloud API costs and latency.
source_title: Kyutai STT & TTS - A Perfect Local Voice Solution?

claim: Kyutai TTS supports voice cloning from 10-second voice samples; quality voice embeddings enable realistic voice synthesis with custom intonation and characteristics.
actionable: For voice cloning in TTS, collect 10-second speaker samples and use Kyutai; supports real-time synthesis with minimal latency.
source_title: Kyutai STT & TTS - A Perfect Local Voice Solution?

claim: Kyutai does not release voice embedding models for privacy reasons (to prevent non-consensual cloning); only provides pre-made voice embeds from public datasets.
actionable: If building voice cloning apps, be aware Kyutai's embedding model is not available; you must either use their pre-made voices or find workarounds.
source_title: Kyutai STT & TTS - A Perfect Local Voice Solution?

claim: Kyutai TTS trained on 2.5M hours of data, significantly more than competitors; Whisper was used to label the data for training.
actionable: For TTS quality, data scale matters; Kyutai's 2.5M hours of data is a competitive advantage reflected in synthesis quality.
source_title: Kyutai STT & TTS - A Perfect Local Voice Solution?
